# File & Media Module LLM Guide

Purpose: R2-backed file uploads for Cloudflare Workers — portals, avatars,
documents, CMS images, attachments.

Entry points:
- `src/index.ts` exports the safe public surface.
- `src/use-cases/` holds framework-neutral behavior: createUploadTicket,
  completeUpload, expireStaleTickets, deleteFile, listFiles.
- `src/keys.ts` builds tenant-scoped object keys (the isolation source of truth).
- `src/ports/` defines MediaStore and ObjectStorage.
- `src/adapters/` contains D1 + memory metadata stores and R2 + memory object stores.
- `migrations/0001_file_media.sql` owns upload_tickets and media_files.
- `module.json` is the machine-readable manifest.

Model:
- Provider module wrapping R2. Two-step upload: ticket (intent) -> put bytes ->
  complete (verify + record). Object bytes live in R2; D1 holds metadata only.

Correctness invariants (the point of the module — do not remove):
- Keys are tenant-prefixed via buildObjectKey; reads/deletes are tenant-guarded.
- Content type is allow-listed and size-capped before an upload is accepted.
- completeUpload verifies the object exists and fits the size limit before recording.
- expireStaleTickets removes orphaned objects for tickets never completed.
- deleteFile is a soft delete (object removed, record retained).

Agent rules:
- Keep use cases neutral; metadata behind MediaStore, object I/O behind ObjectStorage.
- Never make real I/O in tests; inject the memory adapters.
- Run image variants async via jobs-workflows (onFileUploaded hook), never inline.
- Ask before changing migrations, object deletion, or production deploy.
